November 9,2001
David L. Gould, Treasurer
Rockard J. Delgadillo, Officeholder Account
James Hahn, Mayor
David L. Gould Company
-555 South Flower, Suite 4510
Los Angeles, CA 90071
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. G-01-240
Dear Messrs. Gould, Delgadillo & Hahn:
This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the provisions of the
Political Reform Act (the "Act").1 It appears from your lettei that your question
concerning your prior decisions to create separate officeholder committees relate to past
conduct. Your letter states you are seeking "written confirmation" thatthe advice to your
clients was correct. The Commission's Legal Division may not offer advic e regarding
past conduct, however, and must therefore decline to provide you advice concerning any
decision that pertains to past conduct. (Regulation 18329, copy enclosed.) For your
information, we enclose a copy of the Hicla Advice Letter, r-99-lzo, in which
Commission staff advised how local officeholders may provide for an officeholder
account under the provisions of the Government Code section 85201. To the extent we
provide you this general assistance, this letter does not represent an analysis ofany past
conduct, nor of any laws pertaining to Proposition 34 and local ordinances. Also
enclosed for your information is Regulation 18530.4.
I Govemment Code sections 81000 - 91014. Commission regulations appeal at Title 2, sections
1 8 1 0 9- 1 8 997, of the California Code of Regulations.

sections.conclusion: The Commission's Legal Division may not offer advice regarding past conduct and must decline to provide advice concerning any decision that pertains to past conduct.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether the FPPC can provide written confirmation that prior advice given to clients regarding the creation of separate officeholder committees was correct.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declines to advise on past conduct, as per Regulation 18329. The FPPC provides general reference materials on how local officeholders may establish officeholder accounts under Government Code section 85201, but this does not constitute analysis of past conduct or relevant laws.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a declination/denial letter rather than a standard advice letter. No traditional QUESTION/CONCLUSION sections present; question and conclusion extracted from body text. Document references Regulation 18329 which prohibits advice on past conduct.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether the FPPC can provide written confirmation that prior advice given to clients regarding the creation of separate officeholder committees was correct.

CONCLUSION: The Commission's Legal Division may not offer advice regarding past conduct and must decline to provide advice concerning any decision that pertains to past conduct.

embedding.summary: The FPPC declines a request for written confirmation that prior advice regarding officeholder committee creation was correct, citing its prohibition on advising regarding past conduct per Regulation 18329. The FPPC provides reference materials on officeholder accounts for informational purposes.
